arts.ttl: Celtic starting XI to face Dortmund revealed as Reo Hatate decision made and Auston Trusty gets Champions League nod
arts.sum: Bernardo - on target in the 6-0 win in Perth against St Johnstone - retains his place with team-mate Hatate having to settle for a place on the bench once again alongside former Dundee midfielder Luke McCowan. Celtic put on a five-star show on matchday one of the Champions League to dump Slovan Bratislava at Parkhead, but this match brings a much tougher test for the Hoops in terms of standard and quality, but it is one Rodgers and his troops are buzzing for.

arts.ttl: Confirmed lineups: Borussia Dortmund vs. Celtic FC
arts.sum: Official team news has just been released for Die Schwarzgelben who welcome Scottish champions Celtic to the Westfalenstadion for Matchday 2 of the UEFA Champions League.

arts.ttl: Brendan Rodgers singles out 'quality' Dortmund player as Celtic prepare for Champions League clash
arts.sum: Speaking to official Celtic media channels, Rodgers admitted that he is looking forward to seeing Dortmund midfielder Emre Can, another of his former players while in charge of Liverpool. He stated: “I’m looking forward to seeing Emre Can. Emre was a player that was absolutely brilliant for me at Liverpool.

arts.ttl: Borussia Dortmund vs Celtic LIVE as Paulo Bernardo starts and Kyogo leads the line
arts.sum: Brendan Rodgers’ side are unbeaten this season but face their toughest test yet in front of the famed Yellow Wall

arts.ttl: Darren O'Dea shares exactly where 'fantastic' Celtic start under Brendan Rodgers has stemmed from
arts.sum: Celtic have been imperious under Brendan Rodgers this season, and there seems to be no stopping the Scottish Premiership champions in domestic action. “Anyone who has been in pre-season knows you can’t judge too much off it and you are nervous that because things were going so well, are we missing something here?

arts.ttl: Who sang ‘You’ll Never Walk Alone’ first: Liverpool, Celtic or Borussia Dortmund? Origins and history
arts.sum: Curiously, Liverpool won the tie 2-1 and booked their place in their first European final where they would meet Borussia Dortmund - the only other club that has adopted You’ll Never Walk Alone – in the mid-90s. It is believed that Celtic supporters first started singing You’ll Never Walk Alone after a European Cup Winners’ Cup semi-final tie against Liverpool on 19 April 1966 .

arts.ttl: Luis Palma 'makes decision' on Celtic future after summit as £13.3m January transfer splurge gets green light
arts.sum: The winger has struggled for regular game time so far this season

arts.ttl: The Champions League 'guest' Darren O'Dea invited to check on two Celtic players in win vs Slovan Bratislava
arts.sum: And speaking about the Irish players in Celtic’s team, the Player Pathway Manager said he invited John O’Shea to cast his eye over Adam Idah and Liam Scales for the upcoming Republic of Ireland internationals this month. Celtic currently sit second in the league to Bayern Munich on goal difference after their impressive 5-1 win over Slovan Bratislava on matchday one that left pundits and former players waxing lyrical over Rodgers’ team.
